data.title: BranchOut Food Signs At-The-Market Issuance Sales Agreement With Alexander Capital
data.description:
BranchOut Food entered into an At-The-Market Issuance Sales Agreement to offer and sell up to $1.5 million of its common stock through Alexander Capital as sales agent. Shares may be sold from time to time at prevailing market prices, with Alexander Capital earning a 3.0% commission on gross proceeds. The program provides BranchOut Food with flexible access to equity capital and can be terminated at any time by either party or upon the sale of all shares under the program.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: At-The-Market Issuance Sales Agreement for up to $1.5 million of common stock
  • Counterparty: Alexander Capital
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 27 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Provide flexible access to equity capital for corporate needs

Original SEC Filing: BranchOut Food Inc. [ BOF ] - 8-K - Jan. 27, 2026

data.title: Provident Financial Q4 revenue beats expectations
data.description:

data.title: Skkynet Cloud Systems, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Skkynet Cloud Systems, Inc., headquartered in Mississauga, Canada, has released its annual 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company, known for its secure connectivity and data acquisition solutions for industrial and office hardware, continues to focus on integrating cloud-based systems to enhance its offerings in the Industrial Internet of Things (IoT) market, also known as Industry 4.0.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $2.55 million, reflecting a slight decrease from the previous year due to a shift from one-time license payments to subscription payments.
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(6,488), a decrease from the previous year's income of $58,697, attributed to increased operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $35,351, down from $99,090 in the previous year, impacted by the transition to subscription sales.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share to Common Shareholders, Basic: $0.00, consistent with the previous year, reflecting the impact of preferred dividends.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share to Common Shareholders, Diluted: $0.00, consistent with the previous year, indicating no significant change in share dilution impact.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Skkynet operates through its subsidiaries to provide secure connectivity and data acquisition solutions for industrial and office hardware. The company focuses on integrating cloud-based systems to enhance its offerings in the Industrial Internet of Things (IoT) market.
  • Product and Service Offerings: Skkynet offers a range of software products, including the Cogent DataHub™ service for Microsoft Azure, which provides real-time data communication and secure connections between machines and applications. The company's software is modular, allowing for customization through Product Packs and Add-ons.
  • Customer Segments: The company serves over 3,000 customers across various industries, including Aerospace, Automation & Control, Chemicals, Communications, Education, Engineering, Energy & Utilities, Financial, Food & Beverage, Government & Municipal, Healthcare & Pharmaceutical, Instrumentation, Manufacturing, Natural Resources, and System Integrators.
  • Revenue Segments: More than 70% of Skkynet's revenues are derived from software licensing for industrial automation systems, with the remainder coming from financial trading, software support, custom development, and legacy system support.
  • Geographical Performance: Skkynet has a global presence with approximately 50 technical partners and resellers in different geographic areas, including partnerships with major companies like Microsoft, Siemens, and AVEVA.
  • Operational Highlights: The company has introduced innovations such as high-speed redundancy facilities, web-based user interfaces, and high-speed storage capabilities. Skkynet's patented technologies address data transmission challenges in cloud-based systems, ensuring security and efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: Skkynet plans to expand its business in three primary areas: real-time data software for industrial automation, cloud-based SaaS products, and new business lines such as AI-focused providers and embedded products. The company aims to improve its cloud services and explore new markets with dedicated marketing and sales efforts.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Skkynet is focusing on expanding its business through the planned expansion of marketing in new markets, including Cloud industrial middleware, Cloud financial services, home monitoring, fleet tracking, and energy usage monitoring. The company is also working with Japanese companies to penetrate the embedded device manufacturing market, aiming to have its software installed directly on electronic devices.
  • Capital Management: The company has been self-financing through positive cash flows from operating activities, with net cash provided by operating activities amounting to $276,827 in 2025. Skkynet is attempting to secure additional capital from independent sources in the form of equity and debt to support its expansion plans. As of October 31, 2025, the company had working capital of $1,027,715, with a current ratio of 2.28 to 1. The company has not declared or paid any cash dividends on its common stock, opting to retain future earnings to finance business expansion. Dividends of 6% are being accrued on Series B preferred shares.
  • Future Outlook: Skkynet plans to continue expanding into new markets, which will require additional cash resources. The company intends to secure specific amounts of financing for the development of each market application before beginning marketing efforts. The company is also focused on increasing subscription revenue, which has grown by 268% in 2025, as part of its strategic shift from perpetual revenue. The company received a $1.9 million funding initiative for Industrial AI Product Development, which includes an interest-free repayable loan, indicating a focus on innovation and technology development.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Challenges: Skkynet faces challenges related to the geographical relocation of manufacturing facilities, which necessitates robust remote monitoring capabilities to maintain safety, confidentiality, and integrity of processes. The company also identifies a growing need for secure information sharing with third parties and AI applications, which could pose operational and security challenges.
  • COVID-19 Impact: The COVID-19 pandemic is highlighted as an uncontrollable event that could negatively impact operations by limiting access to customers, management, and support staff. The company acknowledges the difficulty in measuring the pandemic's impact on operations and financial condition.
  • Financial Challenges: The shift from perpetual to subscription revenue models presents a financial challenge, impacting near-term revenue. The company also notes the need for additional cash resources to expand into new markets, which is contingent on securing financing. The expansion into Cloud services and AI applications is seen as a growth opportunity but requires careful management of market entry and additional staffing.

SEC Filing: Skkynet Cloud Systems, Inc. [ SKKY ] - 10-K - Jan. 27, 2026

data.title: Trump: we'll work out something with South Korea on tariff hike, Yonhap says
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said the United States and South Korea will work out a solution when asked about his Monday announcement he would increase import tariffs on South Korean goods, South Korea's Yonhap news reported on Wednesday.

His announcement on social media that tariffs against South Korean imports including autos will be raised to 25% because the Asian ally's parliament had not lived up its part of a deal he had reached with its president.

"We'll work something out with South Korea," Yonhap quoted Trump as telling reporters at the White House before leaving for Iowa.

The news rattled officials in Seoul who said they were caught by surprise and left them scrambling to find a response to what could be a blow for the export-heavy country that agreed to pour large investments into the U.S. in return for tariff cuts.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Slips Following Trump Comments — Market Talk
data.description: Before heading to the podium to speak in Clive, Iowa, President Trump commented on the U.S. dollar. In answering a question about his thoughts on the recent decline of the dollar, Trump told reporters that the "the dollar's doing great." "Look at the business we're doing, the dollar's doing great," he said. The U.S. dollar index sank to its lowest level in four years today, and Trump's comments appear to be putting further gas into the "Sell America" trade. The decline of the USD, which has slid 2.4% in the past month, has been powering surges in precious metals to record-high prices. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Stifel Financial Declares Three-for-Two Stock Split, Raises Dividend by 11%
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Stifel Financial declared a three-for-two stock split and raised its dividend by 11%.

The financial services firm said Tuesday it will distribute a 50% stock dividend on Feb. 26 to shareholders of record on Feb. 12, with shareholders receiving one additional share of its common stock for every two shares owned.

Cash will be distributed in lieu of fractional shares based on the closing price on the record date, the company said. After the split, the company will have about 155 million shares outstanding, compared with about 103 million shares outstanding currently.

The company also raised its quarterly dividend to 51 cents a share, from 46 cents a share.

The new payout, equal to $2.04 a year, represents an annual yield of about 1.6% based on Tuesday's closing price of $126.34.

The dividend is payable on March 16 to stockholders of record on March 2.

Following the stock split, the 2026 quarterly dividend equates to 34 cents per share.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Nifty Gateway promises permanent NFT hosting in shutdown update
data.description:
Nifty Gateway has outlined new details on how it plans to preserve users' NFTs as it winds down operations, pledging permanent hosting for token metadata and media even after the platform closes.

In a Tuesday post on X, the Gemini-owned NFT marketplace said it intends to migrate most NFT metadata and media hosting to a decentralized storage network, allowing collectors continued access to their assets in perpetuity after the shutdown.

The update follows last week’s announcement that Nifty Gateway would cease operations amid a prolonged downturn in the non-fungible token market.

According to the statement, Nifty Gateway plans to move metadata and media to Arweave, a blockchain-based data storage network designed for permanent archival. However, the company said a subset of NFTs minted in 2021 or earlier have metadata that is permanently linked to Nifty Gateway’s own servers. For those assets, the firm said it will "continue to host the metadata indefinitely."

The platform also extended the deadline for users to withdraw their NFTs, giving customers a 90-day window to move assets off the platform by April 23. After that date, Nifty Gateway said it will continue working with customers to facilitate withdrawals if needed.

In addition, the company said it is developing a bulk withdrawal tool for users with multiple NFTs, which it plans to roll out with sufficient time for customers to use ahead of the April deadline.

Nifty Gateway, which was acquired by Gemini in 2019, entered withdrawal-only mode earlier this year. The platform rose to prominence during the 2021 NFT art boom but has struggled alongside a broader collapse in NFT trading volumes and marketplace activity.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

data.title: PPG Industries sees soft European, industrial markets in 2026 after Q4 profit miss
data.description:
Global paint supplier PPG Industries said on Tuesday it expects demand in Europe and across global industrial end-use markets to remain soft in the coming year, after the company fell short of Wall Street's fourth-quarter profit expectations.

Higher feedstock and energy costs as well as an uncertain macro environment have weighed on demand for companies such as PPG, which serve an array of end markets such as autos, building and construction.

U.S. manufacturing activity slumped to a 14-month low in the fourth quarter, with new orders contracting further and input costs grinding higher as the sector continued to bear the imprint of President Donald Trump's import tariffs.

A slowdown in home construction and purchases has reduced demand for building materials such as paints and coatings, weighing on the bottom line of companies like PPG Industries.

The company also expects profit growth to be weighted toward the second half of 2026, with flat to low-single-digit percentage growth in the first half of the year and increasing to high-single-digit percentage growth in the second half of the year.

PPG expects $50 million in savings in 2026 as it moves ahead with consolidating its European manufacturing operations and other structural reductions.

However, total net sales rose to $3.91 billion in the fourth quarter, from $3.73 billion a year ago.

Net sales in PPG's performance coatings segment rose to $1.32 billion in the October–December quarter from $1.26 billion a year earlier, while sales in its industrial coatings segment increased 3.47% to $1.64 billion, helped by higher selling prices and a favorable currency impact, though weaker sales volumes limited the gain.

The Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania-based company reported adjusted profit of $1.51 per share for the quarter ended December 31, compared with the analysts' average estimate of $1.58 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.
